source.title: Demo reviewed fever headache case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Fever, headache, and neck stiffness
teaching_points: Fever, severe headache, altered mental status, neck stiffness, and inflammatory CSF findings require urgent treatment.
A normal head CT does not end the evaluation when the exam points to central nervous system infection.
Antimicrobial timing matters when this diagnosis is plausible.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient has a severe generalized headache with fever and photophobia.

facts.label: Illness course
facts.value: Symptoms progressed over one day, and her roommate reports she became confused this morning.

provenance.quote: One day progression; confused this morning per roommate.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: She has nausea and light sensitivity but no focal weakness or seizure activity.

provenance.quote: Nausea and photophobia; no focal weakness or seizures.

facts.label: Immunization history
facts.value: Vaccination history is incomplete after moving for college.

provenance.quote: Incomplete vaccination history after moving for college.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Temperature is 39.3 C, heart rate 122/min, blood pressure 102/64 mmHg, respiratory rate 22/min, oxygen saturation 98% on room air.

facts.label: Neurologic exam
facts.value: She is drowsy but arousable, has neck stiffness, and moves all extremities symmetrically.

provenance.quote: Drowsy but arousable; neck stiffness; symmetric movement.

facts.label: CBC
facts.value: White blood cell count is 18.9 x 10^9/L with neutrophil predominance.

facts.label: CSF profile
facts.value: Cerebrospinal fluid has 1,420 white cells/uL with neutrophil predominance, glucose 28 mg/dL, and protein 186 mg/dL.

provenance.quote: CSF WBC 1420/uL neutrophil predominant, glucose 28, protein 186.

facts.label: Gram stain
facts.value: CSF Gram stain shows gram-negative diplococci.

provenance.quote: Gram-negative diplococci seen on CSF Gram stain.

facts.label: Head CT
facts.value: Noncontrast head CT shows no mass effect, hemorrhage, or hydrocephalus.

provenance.quote: No mass effect, hemorrhage, or hydrocephalus.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A rapidly progressive central nervous system infection must be treated as time-sensitive.

provenance.quote: Fever, headache, neck stiffness, altered mental status, and inflammatory CSF require urgent treatment.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Prioritize the combination of fever, mental status change, neck stiffness, and the spinal fluid pattern.

provenance.quote: Fever, altered mental status, neck stiffness, and CSF profile are key.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Bacterial meningitis supported by fever, altered mental status, neck stiffness, inflammatory CSF profile, and Gram stain.
